articles.title: Hormuz Deadline Looms as IMF Warns Oil Shock Drives All Roads to Stagflation
articles.expand: The global financial markets are treading water as the Hormuz reopening deadline looms, with U.S. Donald Trump warning of severe escalation if Iran does not comply. The mood across financial markets is one of “calculated dread,” as investors await a decisive signal while avoiding aggressive positioning. Equities are mixed, currencies largely range-...

articles.title: Fed's Williams: Iran war impact to drive up headline inflation
articles.expand: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams told Bloomberg on Tuesday that the impact of the Iran war will drive up headline inflation.

articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) to keep the OCR at 2.25%, with Governor Breman set to update growth and inflation projections.

articles.expand: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are escalating sharply, setting the tone for the trading day—and the clock is now ticking toward President Trump’s 8 PM deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face the threat of widespread destruction of Iran’s electrical and bridge infrastructure. articles.title: USD: Dovish Fed risks and ceasefire repricing – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale’s Kit Juckes discusses Robin Brooks’ view that the Dollar looks significantly overvalued versus G10 rate differentials and could fall sharply on a ceasefire, with Oil tumbling and safe-haven flows reversing.

articles.title: Oil: War risk and supply headlines support prices – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank analysts highlight that Brent has climbed above $111 per barrel as the Iran conflict continues and Trump links any ceasefire deal to free Oil traffic through the Strait of Hormus.

articles.title: DXY: Energy shock keeps Dollar bid – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes that a prolonged energy shock from the Iran conflict heightens financial stability risks and supports the Dollar beyond what rate differentials imply.

articles.title: AUD/USD recovery stalls below 0.6950 with all eyes on the Iran war
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar (AUD) is trading higher against the US Dollar (USD) for the second consecutive day on Tuesday, but the pair failed to find any significant acceptance above 0.6950.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Gradual recovery path tied to Hormuz risks – NBC
articles.expand: National Bank of Canada (NBC) economists Stéfane Marion and Kyle Dahms describe recent Euro (EUR) weakness versus Dollar (USD) after the Iran conflict and energy shock, but keep a recovery bias for EUR/USD.

articles.title: Gold maintains the bearish bias as Trump's "final" deadline nears. What's next?
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW Gold has been consolidating since Thursday’s selloff when Trump disappointed the market in his address to the nation by maintaining the hawkish stance towards Iran and reiterating the 2–3 week timeline for ending the war. We haven’t got any meaningful development in the meantime, although Trump set a “final” deadline for Iran t...

articles.title: Japan’s Katayama: Cost estimates rely on how long Middle East war will last
articles.expand: Japan’s Finance Minister (FM) Satsuki Katayama said during European trading hours on Tuesday that the administration remains focused on mitigating risks to the Japanese economy in the wake of the Middle East war.

articles.title: UK: Energy shock weighs on outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists describe a quiet week in the UK, with Gilt yields easing as markets trimmed Bank of England (BoE) hike expectations after Governor Bailey pushed back on tightening.

articles.title: EUR/CAD rises to near 1.6100 due to hawkish ECB tone
articles.expand: EUR/CAD gains ground as the Euro (EUR) receives support from the hawkish tone of the European Central Bank (ECB). The currency cross is trading around 1.6100 during the European hours on Tuesday.

articles.title: NZD/USD: Tactical support eyed near 0.5630 – DBS
articles.expand: DBS Group Research’s Philip Wee expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand to keep policy on hold at its April 8 meeting, looking through energy-driven inflation. He notes Governor Anna Breman’s conditional hawkishness if inflation expectations de-anchor.

articles.title: Eurozone Sentix Slumps as Recession Risk Returns and Inflation Fears Surge
articles.expand: Eurozone investor sentiment deteriorated sharply in April, with data signaling that the risk of another recession is coming back into focus just as inflation concerns surge again on elevated oil prices. Sentix Investor Confidence dropped from -3.1 to -19.2, well below expectations of -7.5, and marking the weakest reading since April 2025. The break...

articles.title: ECB: Focus on expectations as Oil shock bites – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Chief Economist Dr. Jörg Krämer argues that the European Central Bank should respond to the recent Oil price shock by prioritizing long-term inflation expectations. He notes that higher energy costs are both lifting inflation and weighing on growth, creating a policy dilemma.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls seem hesitant ahead of Trump’s Hormuz deal deadline
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts some dip-buyers near the 1.3900 mark on Tuesday and reverses part of the previous day's slide as fading hopes for a US-Iran ceasefire revive demand for the safe-haven US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: USD/JPY under pressure as markets price in a BoJ rate hike
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan’s communication is becoming a key driver for USD/JPY as markets increasingly price in a possible rate hike. Investors are watching whether the BoJ signals a broader tightening cycle, which could strengthen the yen and push the pair lower.
